opcode.name: OP_PlayerProfile
opcode.comment: 07/14 rotation (was 0x62f0): the ~43KB char profile S>C, once at enter-world. Content-confirmed (carries the char name + identity). This is the self-id/name source — decoded by parse_player_profile (anchor-scan for name), which drives SpawnShell::zoneEntry name-match to adopt the self-spawn id. EQ Legends char profile: hdr race@21/class@25/level@33; bind@39; name via anchor-scan. See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md

opcode.name: OP_TimeOfDay
opcode.comment: 07/07 rotation; 8B stock timeOfDayStruct byte-identical (wire-verified 8B); advances at EQ game rate

opcode.name: OP_NewZone
opcode.comment: EQ Legends current zone (S>C, ~340B, once per zone-in). Packed null-terminated short_name + long_name (+ zonefile repeat + binary tail). Confirmed 3-way: guktop/'The City of Guk', nektulos/'Nektulos Forest', unrest/'The Estate of Unrest' — each the correct current zone, each a different length. Decoded by EqlDispatch::newZone -> ZoneMgr::setZoneByName. See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md

opcode.name: OP_ZoneBindMarker
opcode.comment: EQ Legends bind-point marker (14B S>C, once at zone-in). Carries the BIND zone id (u32@6), identical across zones (nektulos-vs-upperguk both read 25) — NOT the current zone. Was mis-labeled OP_NewZone until 2026-07-08. Unwired. See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md

opcode.name: OP_SpawnDoor
opcode.comment: 07/07 rotation (community f-patch id, daemon-confirmed bulk door array 1452/2376B). eql door rows are 132B (1452=11*132, 2376=18*132) vs Live doorStruct 136B: first 88B byte-identical to Live's layout (name[32], y/x/z/heading floats + incline u32 @32, 20B field copy, size u32 @72, doorId/opentype/spawnstate/invertstate @80, zonePoint u32 @84), trailing unknown 44B vs Live's 48B. WIRED 2026-07-13: seq-backend-eql owns a 132B parse_door + the doorStruct size override, and newDoorSpawns strides via door_stride(); modulus gates on 132.

opcode.name: OP_GroundSpawn
opcode.comment: 07/14 id 0x7e02 (adopted from legacy showeqlegends; the old 0x6360 32B record is superseded). S>C broadcast that spawns the zone's GROUND OBJECTS: static interactable placeables (forge/tradeskill containers, decorative racks, rocks) AND pickup-able ground-loot items you walk over and loot (the earring). 47x across the fight's 12 zones (e.g. IT66=Forge, IT11068=BrownRock) — NOT personal combat loot, which is instanced in EQL and never hits the ground. Variable 61-65B: {u32 dropId, NUL-term actorDef model @4 e.g. IT63_ACTORDEF, fields, f32 x/y/z at nul+29, u32}. C>S copy = the mouse-drop request, filtered by DIR_Server. Decoded by seq-backend-eql parse_ground_spawn -> SpawnShell::newGroundItem. id_file = actorDef model; the readable item name (via OP_ItemPacket 0x4d7e correlation) + tagging static objects vs pickup-loot are future refinements.

opcode.name: OP_SendZonePoints
opcode.comment: Coords in a zone that will port you to another zone

opcode.name: OP_AAExpUpdate
opcode.comment: 07/09 l-patch correction: the AA-exp bar rides 0x42d1 (12B altExpUpdateStruct), NOT 0x6801 — the ids were CROSS-WIRED with OP_ExpUpdate (the 12B/16B sizes now match their structs). eql layout: u32 altexp (0-100000 per-AA-point progress), u32 aapoints (unspent AA), u32 tail (Live carries u8 percent + pad there; handler reads neither). WIRED 2026-07-13 straight to Player::updateAltExp — the handler is 0-100000-native since ac918ec, so the old 'needs a ->330 conversion' deferral was stale. Size gate declared in seq-backend-eql size_overrides().

opcode.name: OP_ExpUpdate
opcode.comment: 07/09 l-patch correction: the regular exp bar rides 0x6801 (16B expUpdateStruct), NOT 0x42d1 — the ids were CROSS-WIRED with OP_AAExpUpdate. eql layout matches expUpdateStruct: u32 exp (0-100000 permille, in-game-calibrated against the exp bar), u32 0, u32 aaUnspent (@8, not Live's type), u32 0. exp@0 feeds Player::updateExp directly — the daemon already works in 0-100000, so NO scale conversion. Verified: exp wraps once at the level-up in the death-respawn capture (99.459%->3.622%).

opcode.name: OP_GuildMOTD
opcode.comment: 07/07 rotation (community f-patch id, daemon-confirmed): stock guildMOTDStruct byte-perfect (target@8, sender@72, message@140; 656B = header + msg buffer). Present 1×656B in captures. NOT wired yet — named for logs; handler wiring is guild-tier work.

opcode.name: OP_ClientUpdate
opcode.comment: 07/14 rotation (was 0x7171): whole position channel rotated id AND wire size. S>C 24B (was 28B) = OTHER-spawn position broadcast, 19-bit ×8 packed in each word's LOW bits (z rides bits13-31 of the @8 word; y@16 low19; x@20 low19) — re-cracked 2026-07-14 vs the OP_MobUpdate ground-truth stream + self-trajectory smoothness; WIRED S>C (playerUpdateOther → SpawnShell::moveSpawn). C>S 38B (was 42B) self-position — re-cracked 2026-07-14 vs a /loc ground-truth capture: IEEE floats gameX@14/gameY@26/gameZ@10 + heading@18 (low 12b); NO spawnId (client's self report carries none — self-id adoption is via SpawnShell::zoneEntry name-match). WIRED C>S (playerUpdateSelf → Player::applySelfPosition). See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md

opcode.name: OP_SelfPos
opcode.comment: Second EQL self-pos C>S opcode, distinct from OP_ClientUpdate 0x5188: a position-history breadcrumb — N×17B records {y@0/x@4/z@8 floats, seq@12, u32 ts@13} + 1 trailing byte (18B=1 sample up to 2415B=142). Characterized 2026-07-17 vs a /loc capture (all 3 points exact). Variable size → no fixed struct. Decoder is Rust (parse_self_pos_breadcrumb, exposed via the decode_self_pos_breadcrumb FFI) — opcode mapped + decodable, but intentionally NOT wired to a handler: a movement-trail overlay was built end-to-end then removed (laggy, low value; redundant with 0x5188 for position). Xerxes maps this same id; his playerSelfState len==38 gate skips it. See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md

opcode.name: OP_ZoneEntry
opcode.comment: EQ Legends per-spawn zone stream (S>C, one spawn per payload): null-term name + variable block; position = three u32 words at the block tail (Z/Y/X @ len-95/-91/-87), each a signed 19-bit fixed-point (x8) coord in the word's low bits — same packing as Live spawnStruct position words. NAMED OP_ZoneEntry per stock SEQ: the s2c OP_ZoneEntry has carried the per-spawn payload (one fillSpawnStruct per packet) since 2008; OP_ZoneSpawns is the long-dead bulk-array opcode. Decoded by EqlDispatch::spawn. See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md

opcode.name: OP_NpcMoveUpdate
opcode.comment: EQ Legends per-NPC movement stream (S>C, variable 15-21B). Byte-identical bit format to Live OP_NpcMoveUpdate (16b BE spawnId + 16b pad + 6b fieldmask + 19/19/19 y/x/z sign-magnitude >>3 + 12b heading + optional deltas); decoded by the shared decode_npc_move_update. Confirmed 2026-07-08: all 51 ids + positions match the 0x67e0 OP_MobUpdate stream. Prior 917c was a dead mapping (never seen on the wire). See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md.

opcode.name: OP_MobUpdate
opcode.comment: EQ Legends per-mob position update (S>C, 14B). Byte-identical to Live spawnPositionUpdate (packed y:19/z:19/u3:7/x:19/heading:12 fixed-point x8, spawnId u16@0 + 2 zero bytes); decoded by the shared decode_mob_update. The earlier i16-offset read (Y@4/8, Z@6/64, X@10) was a truncated window of these bitfields — the source of the 8192-unit north<->south Y wrap. See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md.

opcode.name: OP_LoadoutSwap
opcode.comment: EQ Legends multiclass loadout swap (S>C, variable). Header u32 spawnId + u8 + u16 innerLen + ZoneEntry-format record (+ serialized inventory tail on the self variant). Self ~118KB (acting client's own refresh); broadcast ~490B (nearby player's swap, no tail). No OP_PlayerProfile follows a swap, so this is the sole source for the new class + level. Decoded by decode_loadout_swap (reuses the 0x4606 record parser); self -> Player::setIdentity, broadcast -> SpawnShell::updateSpawnIdentity. Confirmed firing 2026-07-11 (login-zone/levelup captures, 486-491B short variant). Credit: Xerxes (forums). See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md.

opcode.name: OP_DeleteSpawn
opcode.comment: EQ Legends spawn removal / death (S>C, 4B {u32 spawnId}, deleteSpawnStruct). CONFIRMED 2026-07-08 by time-correlation: 8/9 ids stop receiving OP_MobUpdate position updates at/after this event (mobs killed over a combat session). Lights up the already-wired SpawnShell::deleteSpawn. NOTE: 0x67a8 is the LOOK-ALIKE 4B {id} op but is combat engage/disengage (ids keep moving after; id=0 clears) — NOT despawn. See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md

opcode.name: OP_RemoveSpawn
opcode.comment: 07/07 rotation (community f-patch id, daemon-confirmed): S>C 5B + C>S 4B removeSpawnStruct, none-gated. Present 78×5B in upperguk capture. Wired SpawnShell::removeSpawn.

opcode.name: OP_Death
opcode.comment: EQ Legends death/corpse (S>C, 40B = newCorpseStruct, n=8). CONFIRMED 2026-07-08: decodes field-for-field — victim u32@0 (all 8 ∈ the OP_DeleteSpawn 0x59a1 set), killer u32@4 (=player 13167 every kill), corpse type i32@12, killing-blow spellId u32@16 (-1=melee), damage u32@24 (9-80). Lights up the already-wired SpawnShell::killSpawn → SpawnKilled. Fires just before the matching OP_DeleteSpawn. See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md

opcode.name: OP_WearChange
opcode.comment: EQ Legends equip-change broadcast (32B), decoded but wired INERT (l-patch addendum 3). Layout: {u32 spawnId, u32 wearSlot (7=primary/8=secondary/9), u32 material, u8[20] 0}. S>C = other spawns' equip changes; C>S = the client's own. Stock ShowEQ's WearChange handler only acts on an HP subcommand (shows nothing for wear), so this is NAMED-but-INERT: NO handler is wired. The Live SpawnUpdateStruct/updateSpawnInfo bindings (still in wire_live.cpp) were REMOVED from wire_eql.cpp because sizeof(SpawnUpdateStruct)==32==this packet's size, so binding them would SZC_Match and mis-decode wear bytes as a spawn update (spawn-state corruption). Each wear event is preceded by a 0x2575/0x2a0a appearance-refresh pair (value 0x40=wear, 0x04=combat/death); own changes are echoed by 0x7820 (~1.1KB item). Live equipment tracking would build on this layout. Named per addendum 3 so it stops showing as unknown in --opcode-stats. See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md.
opcode.priorityNote: stock handler no-op; id mapped for log id only

opcode.name: OP_SpawnAppearance
opcode.comment: SpawnAppearanceCode

opcode.name: OP_SpawnAppearance2
opcode.comment: EQ Legends extended appearance (24B) — byte-identical to Live spawnAppearance2Struct {u32 spawnId, u32 type, u32 value, u8[12] pad}. Confirmed 2026-07-08: 55 spawn ids, type set {5,6,8,22,26,...} matches Live's {5,6,8,22,26,36,42}; type->value fits (type 6->100/110/115, type 43->0/1/2 flag). Wired S>C to the neutral SpawnShell::updateSpawnLock (acts on type 0x2c mob-lock; no-op on other types). C>S half (ClickBuffOff, type 0x24) acknowledged, unwired. See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md

opcode.name: OP_Stamina
opcode.comment: 07/14 id 0x3b0c (adopted from legacy showeqlegends, capture-verified): stock 8B staminaStruct {u32 food, u32 water} — 'ticks till next eat'. Fires 71x S>C at 8B in the fight capture; food/water tick DOWN together (3396->3364->3332, 4596->4564->4532, step 32). Wired Player::updateStamina; size eql-owned in seq-backend-eql size_overrides().

opcode.name: OP_Stance
opcode.comment: EQ Legends active-stance echo (S>C, 4B activateAbilityStruct {u32 abilityId}). One stance active at a time; the server echoes this on each swap (authoritative). Ability ids (stable eqgame.exe GetAbilityName enum): 117 Offense, 118 Defense, 119 Evasive, 120 Balanced, 121 Mage Hunter, 122 Striker, 123 Berserker, 124 Ranged, 135 Channeler. Decoded by decode_activate_ability -> EqlDispatch::stance -> Player::setStance -> PlayerStats.stance. Size eql-owned in seq-backend-eql size_overrides(activateAbilityStruct=4). The C>S activation request is VARIABLE size: 4B, or 9B (ability id + u32 + trailing zero). Three of our captures (7/16, 7/17, and a 7/20 full-sweep) show ONLY the 4B form: the sweep exercised 16 distinct abilities (8 stances + 8 invocations = every one the character had) over 16 requests, all 4B. An external 2026-07-20 capture saw ~half at 9B, so the long form is real but is NOT ability-specific; suspect client build or input method (hotbar vs menu vs command). Untested ids: 123 Berserker, 130 Inviolable, 132 Chained. It is acked uint8_t/none precisely so that variance never warns — do NOT size-match the client dir. Request/response, NOT a group/zone broadcast — CONFIRMED 2026-07-20 by a grouped two-character capture: in the 21s between the client's own last swap and the group disband, the grouped partner swapped twice while the client decoded 620 packets (577 S>C, no silent second) with ZERO 0x0fab/0x3b12 fires. Positive control: the client's own swaps 4s earlier echoed 1:1 with its C>S. So the echo reports ONLY the local character's own stance and cannot track group members'. See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md

opcode.name: OP_Invocation
opcode.comment: EQ Legends active-invocation echo (S>C, 4B activateAbilityStruct {u32 abilityId}). One invocation active at a time; the server echoes this on each swap (authoritative). Ability ids (stable eqgame.exe GetAbilityName enum): 125 Recover, 126 Empower, 127 Inversion, 128 Spell Blade, 129 Over Channel, 130 Inviolable, 131 Divine, 132 Chained, 133 Arcane Mastery, 134 Unyielding. Decoded by decode_activate_ability -> EqlDispatch::invocation -> Player::setInvocation -> PlayerStats.invocation. Same 4B struct as OP_Stance; opcode id picks stance vs invocation. The C>S activation request is VARIABLE size: 4B, or 9B (ability id + u32 + trailing zero). Three of our captures (7/16, 7/17, and a 7/20 full-sweep) show ONLY the 4B form: the sweep exercised 16 distinct abilities (8 stances + 8 invocations = every one the character had) over 16 requests, all 4B. An external 2026-07-20 capture saw ~half at 9B, so the long form is real but is NOT ability-specific; suspect client build or input method (hotbar vs menu vs command). Untested ids: 123 Berserker, 130 Inviolable, 132 Chained. It is acked uint8_t/none precisely so that variance never warns — do NOT size-match the client dir. Request/response, NOT a group/zone broadcast — CONFIRMED 2026-07-20 by a grouped two-character capture: in the 21s between the client's own last swap and the group disband, the grouped partner swapped twice while the client decoded 620 packets (577 S>C, no silent second) with ZERO 0x0fab/0x3b12 fires. Positive control: the client's own swaps 4s earlier echoed 1:1 with its C>S. So the echo reports ONLY the local character's own stance and cannot track group members'. See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md

opcode.name: OP_HPUpdate
opcode.comment: 07/07 rotation; multiplexed stat channel (subtype byte @4). 6B subtype-0x02 = HP-bar feed (u16 id, u16 0, 0x02, u8 hp%); decoded by EqlDispatch::hpUpdate. Other sizes/subtypes are cur/max i64 stat pairs (dropped)

opcode.name: OP_GuildMemberUpdate
opcode.comment: Info regarding guild members

opcode.name: OP_ClickObject
opcode.comment: 07/07 rotation (community f-patch id, wire-verified drop+pickup): stock remDropStruct byte-perfect (u16 dropId matches ground-spawn id, u16 spawnId=player). Match-gated. Wired SpawnShell::removeGroundItem. Not in current fixtures — community-verified.

opcode.name: OP_Action
opcode.comment: EQ Legends spell/special action (S>C). Live paired send: 64B actionStruct (n=97/117) + 88B actionAltStruct (n=22/43); target/source u16@0/@2, spell u16@4 (real ids 502/445/821). Sole 64B/88B op. Lights up the already-wired SpellShell::action (both payloads). See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md

opcode.name: OP_Action2
opcode.comment: EQ Legends melee combat / damage-resolution stream (S>C, 48B fixed, n=481/968). Byte-identical to Live action2Struct: target u16@0, source u16@2, damage i32@8, spell i32@20 (-1=melee), type u8@40; @24-39 = knockback floats. Sole 48B S>C op; cross-val upperguk+fulllogin. Lights up the already-wired CombatRouter::action2. See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md

opcode.name: OP_Consider
opcode.comment: EQ Legends /consider (post-2026-07-07 re-map). 24B {u32 self, u32 target, u32 faction, u32 =7} both directions: C>S request has faction=0, S>C reply fills faction (2=warmly, 4=amiably; level comes from the spawn, not here). considerStruct is eql's OWN 24B struct (seq-backend-eql owns it; Live's is 32B) — size-gated on it via the backend size table (struct_size_overrides), decoded by parse_consider into the shared Consider, then the neutral SpawnShell::consMessage. See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md

opcode.name: OP_TargetMouse
opcode.comment: EQ Legends C>S target select. Payload is byte-identical to Live's clientTargetStruct ({u32 spawn_id}, 0 = clear/untarget), so it wires straight to the neutral SpawnShell::clientTarget — no Legends-specific decode. Confirmed 2026-07-07 across fight + Nektulos locclip captures (every payload resolves to a 0x7475 spawn id or 0). See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md

opcode.name: OP_SpawnRename
opcode.comment: Spawns getting renamed after initial NewSpawn

opcode.name: OP_Illusion
opcode.comment: Spawn being illusioned (changing forms)

opcode.name: OP_Shroud
opcode.comment: Server putting players into shroud form
opcode.priorityNote: deadOnLegends: shrouding does not exist in the Legends client era

opcode.name: OP_ZoneChange
opcode.comment: CLIENT-ONLY zone-change request, 484B fixed (9 fires in a 26min capture, all C>S, never S>C — a zone transition is a session teardown + new session, so there is no server reply). Carries the player's POSITION and no zone id anywhere, so nothing is decodable here beyond the fact that a transition started; zone identity arrives later via OP_NewZone -> setZoneByName. Cannot use Live's 100B zoneChangeStruct (SZC_Match drops a 484B payload) nor ZoneMgr::zoneChange, which decodes that struct — wired uint8_t/none to EqlDispatch::zoneChange -> ZoneMgr::beginZoning. Layout unmapped beyond the length; legacy showeq gates the same id on a 484B all-unknown struct for the same reason. WHY IT IS SAFE despite the flag gating SpawnShell's stale-zone handlers: the bulk spawn stream does NOT pass one — eql routes OP_ZoneEntry -> EqlDispatch::spawn -> upsertSpawn, which is unguarded (legacy is equivalent: its eql spawns go to SpawnShell::zoneEntry, also unguarded, while the isZoning guards sit on newSpawn, which eql never calls). Of the 7 guarded handlers only 3 are wired on eql, and one of those (OP_ClickObject) is still ffff. Measured across the 9 transitions in one capture: 29 of 3880 OP_NpcMoveUpdate and 0 of 23 OP_GroundSpawn fall inside the windows — i.e. it suppresses a little stale movement during a transition, which is what the flag is for, and touches nothing else.
opcode.priorityNote: client-only; handler only raises the zoning flag

opcode.name: OP_GroupInvite
opcode.comment: You invite someone while ungrouped or get invited by someone ungrouped

opcode.name: OP_GroupInvite2
opcode.comment: Invite into an EXISTING group (inviter already grouped); OP_GroupInvite (ee64) is used to form a NEW group. Same groupInviteStruct layout (invitee@0, inviter@64), C>S 168B. Re-confirmed post-2026-05-22 patch (group-invite2: fired only when adding a 3rd member, naming the same inviter + new invitee).
opcode.priorityNote: deadOnLegends: era duplicate - the non-2 group opcodes are mapped and working on Legends

opcode.name: OP_GroupCancelInvite
opcode.comment: Client-side decline-invite OR leave/disband-group request (168 bytes; name fields = self+self when leaving). C>S only in observed captures. Re-confirmed post-2026-05-22 patch (group-2variants: only C>S group-action unknown, fires before disband, self+self).

opcode.name: OP_GroupFollow
opcode.comment: A member joined (168B; invitee name at offset 64, level at 132). Roster identity rides this + OP_GroupDisband; OP_GroupMemberList is dead on Legends.

opcode.name: OP_GroupFollow2
opcode.comment: Player joins your group (168 bytes; member name at offset 64, leading 64 bytes zero, lean tail w/ level field). Re-confirmed post-2026-05-22 patch (group-2variants: fires per form, S>C, simplest of the two form-time structs).
opcode.priorityNote: deadOnLegends: era duplicate - the non-2 group opcodes are mapped and working on Legends

opcode.name: OP_GroupUpdate
opcode.comment: Per-recipient status push (168B fixed, name at offset 64, no member roster); handler noops. Member identity rides OP_GroupFollow, departures OP_GroupDisband/2.

opcode.name: OP_GroupDisband
opcode.comment: Your group dissolved (modern: 168 bytes; membername=self at offset 64)

opcode.name: OP_GroupDisband2
opcode.comment: S>C to all STAYERS when any member departs (not leader-only). 168 bytes; membername=peer at offset 64.
opcode.priorityNote: deadOnLegends: era duplicate - the non-2 group opcodes are mapped and working on Legends

opcode.name: OP_GroupInviteAccepted
opcode.comment: S>C to inviter when invitee accepts ungrouped invite. Fires in same bundle as OP_GroupMemberList + OP_GroupFollow2 (join-complete). 168 bytes; invitee name at offset 64.

opcode.name: OP_GroupInvite2Sent
opcode.comment: S>C to leader immediately when grouped invite (OP_GroupInvite2) is sent — fires before invitee accepts. Leader-only. 168 bytes; both names filled (leader at 0, invitee at 64).

opcode.name: OP_GroupMemberLeave
opcode.comment: S>C broadcast to remaining members when any member leaves. 168 bytes. Fires only when ≥2 members remain after departure; name at offset 64 encodes updated group state.

opcode.name: OP_GroupLeader
opcode.comment: Group leader change (modern: 80 bytes)

opcode.name: OP_Buff
opcode.comment: S>C, two shapes ride this id. (a) 24B COMPACT record {u32 field0@0, u32 spellId@4, u32=1@8, u32 changeType@12, 0, 0}, changeType 4/1. DECODED BUT DELIBERATELY NOT APPLIED — it is probably NOT buff state. In one fixture it carries {1, 665, 4} for "Drifting Death" while that capture's chat reads "a greater kobold has taken 196 damage from your Drifting Death", i.e. a DoT cast ON A MOB, which cannot sit in the player's buff window; another record is {8, 3579} "Share Form of the Great Wolf", also player-cast. So field0 looks like the spell GEM slot cast from, not a buff slot, and values >=15 (15, 331..339 seen) are something else again. Applying it would show spells cast on others as buffs on the player, and treating changeType 1 as a fade would delete a real buff of the same spell id. Legacy showeq buffChange does apply it (unconditional player attribution, add on 4 / delete on 1) and carries that flaw. TO IDENTIFY: cast a known spell from a known gem and see whether field0 tracks the gem, and whether a buff received FROM someone else ever appears here. OP_BuffList/2/3 remain the authoritative buff source. (b) older variable-length forms: 13b fade {u32 spawnID, u32 spellID, u16=0x0001, u8=0, u8 slot, u8=0}; 30b self-buff add; 34/51/53/59b embedded blocks; 85b 3-block. Pre-patch OP_Buff 0x55b9 no longer fires.

opcode.name: OP_BuffList
opcode.comment: Per-spawn active-buff list, resent at zone-in + on every buff change with REAL server-side remaining durations. Xerxes 07/14 map: OP_BuffList/2/3 = 73e2/713a/5c47, all the same format + handler. Variable size: u32 spawnId, u32 (seq/ts), u8 flag=1, u8 count, u8[5] pad, then count records {u32 spellId, u32=1, i32 remainingTicks, u32=0} + NUL caster name + slot (u32 between records, u16 on the final). remainingTicks<=0 = permanent. Wired → SpellShell::buffList; owner by findability (self → buff panel, findable mob → target effects).

opcode.name: OP_BuffList2
opcode.comment: Buff-list variant — same format + handler as OP_BuffList (73e2); see it for the record layout.

opcode.name: OP_BuffList3
opcode.comment: Buff-list variant — same format + handler as OP_BuffList (73e2); see it for the record layout.

opcode.name: OP_Damage
opcode.comment: Combat floating-text feed (S>C), NAMED-INERT — NOT wired: the combat log already comes from OP_Action2 (0x5446, structured); this is the client's display-text subset and would duplicate it. Layout: {u32 sourceSpawn@0, u8 type@4, u32 targetSpawn@5, u32 msgCategory@9, NUL-terminated tail}. type table: 5=normal damage (tail=amount 5-413), 6=CRITICAL (big/rare, up to ~1020), 7=fixed-value damage ~197 (DoT tick), 8=misc damage, 3/4=non-damage (empty tail — miss/absorb), 0/1=spell event (tail=spell link + name). @9 is a filter/color id keyed to event KIND (15566=damage, 15562=miss, spell=12478/439/…), constant within damage — so crit is the TYPE byte (6), not @9. Kept for a future floating-combat-text overlay.

opcode.name: OP_BeginCast
opcode.comment: BeginCastCode - S>C broadcast: {u32 spellId, u16 casterSpawnId, u16 castTime_ms, u8[7] pad}. Re-confirmed post-2026-05-22 patch (castTime decodes to real ms: 4000/6500/9000).

opcode.name: OP_CastSpell
opcode.comment: 07/09 l-patch: the player's own cast request (C>S, 40B) = stock startCastStruct {i32 gemSlot@0, u32 spellId@4, u8[10]@8, u32 targetId@18, ...} + 1 trailing byte. Carries the target, so casts show in the spell-timer window immediately with the target. Wired → SpellShell::selfStartSpellCast; 40B via a startCastStruct size override (see seq-backend-eql size_overrides).

opcode.name: OP_SwapSpell
opcode.comment: 07/07 rotation (community f-patch id, wire-verified): stock tradeSpellBookSlotsStruct (slot1 u32@0, slot2 u32@4) 8B exact. Match-gated. NOT wired yet — named for logs; not in current fixtures.

opcode.name: OP_MemorizeSpell
opcode.comment: Bidirectional 16B: {u32 gem_slot, u32 spellId, int16 action, int16 pad, u8[4] unknown}. action=1 C>S begin memorizing (no ack); action=2 C>S+S>C memorize complete (client shows 'You have finished memorizing'); action=3 C>S+S>C zone-in spell sync (declare existing spell in slot); action=4 observed post-patch (gem clear/forget, semantics TBD). Re-confirmed post-2026-05-22 patch: 11 C>S + 11 S>C balanced.
opcode.priorityNote: deadOnLegends: rides the client dir of OP_Buff

opcode.name: OP_InspectAnswer
opcode.comment: 07/07 rotation (community f-patch id): stock inspectDataStruct byte-perfect incl. 1956B total size (3/3 community inspects). Match-gated (dark if size differs). Wired MessageShell::inspectData. Not in current fixtures — community-verified.

opcode.name: OP_Emote
opcode.comment: 07/09 EQL (community f-patch id): 4B header (ffffffff) + emote text; client is a fixed 1028B buffer, server dir is variable, so none-gated. NOT wired yet — named for logs (emote also rides OP_FormattedMessage which is handled).

opcode.name: OP_SimpleMessage
opcode.comment: EQL 07/12 rotation; single general SimpleMessage channel (12B server: u32 eqstrId, u32 color, u32 0). Wire-verified via the fast-camp-refusal string (eqstr 10031, color 13); replay opcodestats show it as 12B S>C across every system-message capture. Was pinned to the dead live opcode ffff; the MessageShell/SpellShell handlers were already correct

opcode.name: OP_LootDrops
opcode.comment: eql corpse loot window: {u16, u32=900, u32 corpseId@6, u32 count@10, NUL-term corpse name@14, then `count` item entries each with a 0x12 item link}. Decoded by parse_loot_drops (item name = link past 197-hex header) -> MessageShell::lootDrops -> LootDrops proto.

opcode.name: OP_Unknown4
opcode.comment: NOT money — was misnamed OP_MoneyUpdate until 2026-07-19; the real one is 0x6414. 12B {u32 @0, u32=1, u32=42}, S>C, fires on a 60s timer (26 fires at 60.0s +/-0.9s across one 26min capture), so it is a heartbeat, not an event. Field @0 read 0 in 13 of 26 fires while the character carried 9,275,933 copper, and otherwise drifted in a narrow 11649..12098 band unrelated to the purse — it is not a coin total in any denomination. Deliberately UNWIRED. Meaning of @0 still unknown.

opcode.name: OP_LootTransaction
opcode.comment: Subcode-multiplexed corpse-loot channel; the client requests a loot action and the server confirms. Subcodes (u16@0) seen in a 26min capture: 3 (C 2B / S 6B request+ack, purpose unknown), 6 (C 25B, the loot request), 5 (S 16B, layout unmapped), 7 (S 36B, the confirmation, decoded). The 36B confirmation echoes the request body and appends the proceeds: {u16 subcode=7@0, u16=1@2, u32 itemId@4, u32 slot@8, u32 corpseId@12, u32 quantity@16, u32 requestSeq@20, u16@24, u32 coinCopper@26, u8[6]=0@30}. WIRE-VERIFIED: across 24 confirmations the 18 non-zero @26 values matched exactly and in full the 18 amounts the server separately stated as English text ("...and sold it for 3 gold, 5 silver and 7 copper" = 357), and all 18 distinct corpseIds are spawns that both died and appear as a loot_drops corpse id. Decoded by parse_loot_transaction -> EqlDispatch::lootTransaction -> Player::adjustMoney + proto LootTransaction; this replaced parsing the sale wording out of OP_LootMessage. NOTE legacy showeq maps this id as a death/combat record (deathRecordEQLStruct {sourceId, skillOrDamage, targetId, combatSeq}) and discards it. That reading does not survive the capture: the record is client-initiated, every target was looted rather than merely killed, its trailing field is coin, and its "always 1" field @16 also takes the value 2. Kills are covered separately by OP_Death (0x36bf): 47 fires -> 47 spawn_killed, versus 24 of these.

opcode.name: OP_MoneyUpdate
opcode.comment: Carried purse, 20B S>C {u32 platinum@0, gold@4, silver@8, copper@12, u32=0@16}. Denominations are NOT normalized (9276p 33g 101s 281c captured), so sum rather than assuming each is < 10. Values agree with OP_PlayerProfile's own coin block wherever both appear, verified on two characters. Mostly broadcasts near zone-in, but it DOES also report mid-session changes the profile never sees (one capture: purse moved +12804 while visible auto-sales accounted for only 3142), so it is the authoritative source — though it does not fire per coin-earning event, hence the OP_LootMessage accrual on top. NOTE when counting fires: recon follows the primary box unless --dump-all-sessions is passed. Layout credit: Xerxes (legacy moneyUpdateEQL); the id rotated from 0x4d77 in the 2026-07-14 patch, which is why this sat unmapped.

opcode.name: OP_LootMessage
opcode.comment: eql personal auto-loot/sell text: {u32 color@0, NUL-term text with 0x12 item links (197-hex header + name)}. Decoded by parse_loot_message -> MessageShell::lootMessage -> chat.

opcode.name: OP_FormattedMessage
opcode.comment: 07/14 id 0x15d0 (was 0x3c0a; rotated AND relaid-out): stock length-prefixed FormattedMessage — {u32@0=0, u8@4=0, u32@5=formatId (eqstr_us.txt id), u32@9=msgType/chatColor, @13=length-prefixed [u32 len][bytes] arg slots}. Same arg blob EQStr::formatMessage consumes on Live. Decoded by seq-backend-eql parse_formatted_message (formatId@5, args cleaned) -> MessageShell::formattedMessageEQL interpolates via EQStr + emits chat. Resolves readable msgs incl. level-ups/exp/AA/combat/spell. The old 3c0a flat layout (spellId@0/formatId@9/caret@13) is retired.

opcode.name: OP_CommonMessage
opcode.comment: 07/07 rotation (community f-patch id, daemon-confirmed): /tell /ooc /shout etc, channelMessageStruct none. Present both dirs (63/71B) in captures. Wired MessageShell::channelMessage.

opcode.name: OP_SpecialMesg
opcode.comment: 07/07 rotation (community f-patch id, daemon-confirmed): NPC speech / hail responses, specialMessageStruct none. Present 8×(59/60B) in upperguk capture. Wired MessageShell::specialMessage.

opcode.name: OP_RandomReq
opcode.comment: 07/07 rotation (community f-patch id, wire-verified /random): stock randomReqStruct {u32 bottom, u32 top}, BUT both 8B and 9B forms on the wire (trailing byte optional) so none-gated. NOT wired yet — named for logs.

opcode.name: OP_RandomReply
opcode.comment: 07/07 rotation (community f-patch id, wire-verified /random 1 100 = 63): stock randomStruct {u32 bottom, u32 top, u32 result, char name[64]} 76B exact. Match-gated. NOT wired yet — named for logs.

opcode.name: OP_ConsentResponse
opcode.comment: Server replying with consent information after /consent

opcode.name: OP_DenyResponse
opcode.comment: Server replying with deny information after /deny

opcode.name: OP_ManaChange
opcode.comment: Resource update after spell/ability use (S>C, 20b): {i32 curMana, i32 curEndurance, i32 spellOrAbilityId, u8[8]}. Fires once per cast or combat ability; spellId@8 cross-refs OP_CastSpell. field4 = current endurance, NOT max mana (legacy label wrong) — confirmed 2026-05-26 by cross-ref against OP_EndUpdate cur. EQ Legends id=0x2255, re-confirmed post-remap 2026-07-20 against 7/14+ captures ONLY: eql-fighting 467 fires all S>C all 20B, eqlegends-patch20260714 1 fire S>C 20B — byte-identical to manaDecrementStruct; lights up the already-wired Player::manaChange. Carries CURRENT mana only, NO max — the stat-sync wide form is the only max source. (Pre-remap id was 0x07c9, dead since the 2026-07-14 rotation; the 2026-07-08 value-match established the struct semantics, not this id.) See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md.

opcode.name: OP_BazaarSearchRequest
opcode.comment: Bazaar search request

opcode.name: OP_BazaarSearchResponse
opcode.comment: Bazaar search Response - Struct incorrect

opcode.name: OP_MoneyOnCorpse
opcode.comment: MoneyOnCorpseCode

opcode.name: OP_SkillUpdate
opcode.comment: 07/14 id 0x3873 (adopted from legacy showeqlegends, capture-verified): stock skillIncStruct byte-perfect (12B: skillId u32, value i32, +4). Fires 108x S>C at 12B in the fight capture; sane skillIds (26/52/80/82) with values incrementing on skill-up (244->245, 239->240). Wired Player::increaseSkill.
opcode.priorityNote: prior id 0dc7 was the zone-in bulk dump (OOB crash); bounds guard kept

opcode.name: OP_LevelUpdate
opcode.comment: LevelUpUpdateCode

opcode.name: OP_DzSwitchInfo
opcode.comment: Expedition compass etc
opcode.priorityNote: deadOnLegends: superseded - Legends wire form is mapped and handled as OP_DzSwitchInfoEQL

opcode.name: OP_DzInfo
opcode.comment: Expedition Information
opcode.priorityNote: deadOnLegends: superseded - Legends DZ info is handled via OP_DzSwitchInfoEQL

opcode.name: OP_EnvDamage
opcode.comment: Environmental Damage

opcode.name: OP_SetRunMode
opcode.comment: old cRunToggleCode

opcode.name: OP_Trader
opcode.comment: PC's turning trader on and off

opcode.name: OP_ItemLinkResponse
opcode.comment: ItemInfoCode

opcode.name: OP_BuffFadeMsg
opcode.comment: SpellFadeCode
opcode.priorityNote: deadOnLegends: no fade packet; fades tracked via buff-list updates

opcode.name: OP_CorpseLocResponse
opcode.comment: old CorpseLocCode:

opcode.name: OP_ItemPacket
opcode.comment: EQ Legends bulk item transfer (S>C, ~1.1KB-19KB, sent on zone-in). u32@4 = item count N, then N item records ~1122-1130b each (16-char serial + name×2 + Trophy/Benefit descriptor + stats; item-id candidate = first u32). IDENTIFIED, NOT DECODED — the record layout (3-class fields etc.) is future work, and the Legends format differs from Live's itemPacketStruct, so this is intentionally NOT wired to the itemCache sink. See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md.

opcode.name: OP_LeaderExpUpdate
opcode.comment: Leadership AA Exp Update

opcode.name: OP_MovementHistory
opcode.comment: Movement history for speed/movement hack detection

opcode.name: OP_UIUpdate
opcode.comment: Seems to be sent to handle a variety of UI updates - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_GroupMemberInfo
opcode.comment: Recipient-only group event notification (modern: 168 bytes; recipient name@64, leading 64b zero, tail u32 epoch + group_id + spawn_id fields). NOT the actual roster — that's OP_GroupMemberList=0x312a. Misidentified previously based on 'fires per form-event' shape alone; content fingerprint shows only the recipient's own name with no peer identity. Kept named distinctly to avoid confusion; needs a real purpose attribution.

opcode.name: OP_GroupMemberList
opcode.comment: Modern group roster broadcast. Variable length (54-301b observed: 1-2 members at 54-107b, 6-member group at 301b). Wire layout: {u32 groupId, u32 memberCount} header, then a slot-0 leader extended record (2 null-padded name fields + u32 level/class + flags), then (memberCount-1) regular slot records each prefixed with u32 slotIdx (1, 2, …) and a null-padded name. Fires S>C at zone-in (existing roster recovery) AND on every roster mutation. Confirmed against groupbuff-repro.vpk (6-member, leader name + 5 slot markers found at 48-byte intervals starting at offset 0x42) and group-2variants.vpk (1- and 2-member cases).

opcode.name: OP_GuildMemberList
opcode.comment: List of guild members - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_ManaUpdate
opcode.comment: Mana bar update - 10 bytes (spawn_id u16, cur u32, max u32). Per-spawn broadcast (not just local player); local player shows +10/tick regen. Same wire shape as OP_EndUpdate. Re-confirmed post-2026-05-22 patch.

opcode.name: OP_EndUpdate
opcode.comment: Endurance Update opcode - 10 bytes (spawn_id u16, cur u32, max u32)

opcode.name: OP_Marketplace
opcode.comment: Marketplace data - Guessing variable length 11444 bytes as of 11/28/12

opcode.name: OP_MarketplaceSelect
opcode.comment: Sent when highlighting an item in Marketplace - 80 bytes

opcode.name: OP_DzMembers
opcode.comment: Expedition Members - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_Campfire
opcode.comment: Fellowship campfire information - 1076 bytes

opcode.name: OP_SelectCampfire
opcode.comment: Fellowship campfire Choices - Guessing variable length

opcode.name: OP_Claims
opcode.comment: Contents of claims window. /claim then refresh to capture packet - Guessing variable length

opcode.name: OP_VoiceChat
opcode.comment: Voice chat server info - Variable length (Data sent when joining group,raid etc)

opcode.name: OP_PollQuestions
opcode.comment: SOE in-game player poll questions - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_PollResponses
opcode.comment: Poll response choices - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_ShroudProgression
opcode.comment: Unlocked shrouds - 293 bytes

opcode.name: OP_ShroudTemplates
opcode.comment: Shroud templates to choose from on shroud NPC - 18983 bytes

opcode.name: OP_Fellowship
opcode.comment: Fellowship information - 2576 bytes

opcode.name: OP_ExpandedGuildInfo
opcode.comment: Guild ranks and other misc guild data - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_GuildBank
opcode.comment: Guild bank contents - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_OpenTradeskillContainer
opcode.comment: Opens forge, kiln etc - 92 Bytes

opcode.name: OP_TradeskillRecipes
opcode.comment: Learned recipes - 84 Bytes

opcode.name: OP_TradeSkillCombine
opcode.comment: Tradeskill combine using new tradeskill window - 40 Bytes

opcode.name: OP_TradeSkillResult
opcode.comment: Packets sent when tradeskill combine successful equals # of ingredients used in combine?- 12 Bytes

opcode.name: OP_TradeSkillCombineOld
opcode.comment: Tradeskill combine using old tradeskill window - 24 Bytes

opcode.name: OP_ItemPlayerPacket
opcode.comment: Inventory/bank items coming over during zone - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_TaskDescription
opcode.comment: Task descriptions coming down for task window - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_TaskActivity
opcode.comment: Task activity descriptions coming down for task window - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_CompletedTasks
opcode.comment: Task history for task window - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_CustomTitles
opcode.comment: List of available titles - 1520 bytes

opcode.name: OP_Animation
opcode.comment: EQ Legends spawn animation broadcast (S>C, 4B, n=354/718). Byte-identical to Live animationStruct: spawnId u16@0, action u8@2 (1-46), speed u8@3 (=10 across all 354). No handler wired (animation not surfaced) — id remapped so it resolves cleanly in opcode-stats. See OPCODES_LEGENDS.md

opcode.name: OP_WhoAllRequest
opcode.comment: WhoAllReqCode - 156 bytes

opcode.name: OP_WhoAllResponse
opcode.comment: WhoAllOutputCode - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_InspectRequest
opcode.comment: 07/09 EQL (community f-patch id, wire-verified): C>S 8B {u32 targetId, u32 selfId}. Identification-only, no consumer. Named for logs.

opcode.name: OP_GetGuildMOTD
opcode.comment: Request guildMOTD - 648 Bytes

opcode.name: OP_TradeRequest
opcode.comment: You request trade session - 8 bytes

opcode.name: OP_TradeDeny
opcode.comment: Target not ready to trade - 12 bytes

opcode.name: OP_TradeRequestAck
opcode.comment: Trade request recipient is acknowledging they are able to trade - 8 bytes

opcode.name: OP_CancelTrade
opcode.comment: Cancel a trade window - 8 bytes

opcode.name: OP_FinishTrade
opcode.comment: Trade is over - 12 bytes

opcode.name: OP_TradeCoins
opcode.comment: You put coins put in a trade window - 20 bytes

opcode.name: OP_TradeCoins2
opcode.comment: Someone else puts coins put in your trade window - 12 bytes

opcode.name: OP_TradeAcceptClick
opcode.comment: One side clicks Accept on the trade 8 bytes

opcode.name: OP_ItemLinkClick
opcode.comment: Click on itemlinks - 52 bytes

opcode.name: OP_LootRequest
opcode.comment: LootCorpseCode - 4 bytes

opcode.name: OP_LootItem
opcode.comment: Loot item from corpse - 20 bytes

opcode.name: OP_EndLootRequest
opcode.comment: DoneLootingCode - 4 bytes

opcode.name: OP_Track
opcode.comment: Tracking data - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_AutoAttack
opcode.comment: Attack on/off - C>S 4 bytes: {u32 mode} (1=on, 0=off). Fires first of the paired AutoAttack+AutoAttack2 sends.

opcode.name: OP_AutoAttack2
opcode.comment: Attack on/off - C>S 4 bytes: {u32 mode} (1=on, 0=off). Fires second, paired with OP_AutoAttack on every toggle.

opcode.name: OP_ClickDoor
opcode.comment: DoorOpenCode click request from client - 16 bytes

opcode.name: OP_MoveDoor
opcode.comment: DoorClickCode response from server - 2 bytes

opcode.name: OP_MoveItem
opcode.comment: Client moving an item from one slot to another - 28 bytes. Resolved via inventory.opcodestats.txt: 30 C>S fires at exactly 28 bytes during a slot-swap session, no competing unknown opcode at that size+direction. Wire layout: two 12-byte invSlotStruct descriptors + uint32 stack count.

opcode.name: OP_Jump
opcode.comment: JumpCode - 0 bytes

opcode.name: OP_TGB
opcode.comment: Client telling server to set targetgroupbuff - 4 bytes

opcode.name: OP_Lockouts
opcode.comment: Expedition lockouts

opcode.name: OP_RaidInvite
opcode.comment: Client side raid invite requests 140 bytes

opcode.name: OP_RaidJoin
opcode.comment: Server side raid information - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_Feedback
opcode.comment: Client sending feedback to server - 1148 bytes

opcode.name: OP_GuildsList
opcode.comment: Listing of all guilds. Can be triggered by /lfg search?

opcode.name: OP_GuildsInZoneList
opcode.comment: Listing of guild names present in the current zone. Generally seen on zoning

opcode.name: OP_NewGuildInZone
opcode.comment: An additional guild name has appeared in the current zone. Seen when a guilded player enters the current zone

opcode.name: OP_LFGGetMatchesRequest
opcode.comment: LFG/LFP client request - 16 bytes

opcode.name: OP_LFGGetMatchesResponse
opcode.comment: LFG/LFP server response - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_MercenaryList
opcode.comment: Listing of hired mercenaries - 648 bytes

opcode.name: OP_ClickInventory
opcode.comment: Click items from inventory or armor - 16 bytes 5th byte is Slot ID

opcode.name: OP_ClickInventoryAck
opcode.comment: Server acknowledges click from inventory or armor - 20 bytes

opcode.name: OP_BuffQuery
opcode.comment: C>S: {u32 buff_slot, u32 self_spawn_id} (8 bytes). Client requests server reconfirm buff slot N at zone-in. Fires once per occupied buff slot on the SECOND zone-in after a buff was applied (not the first — the buff must survive one full zone cycle before the client requests verification). Server responds with OP_Buff for that slot. Wire-verified: slot=0 → OP_Buff IVU(225) spellslot=0; slot=1 → DE(352) spellslot=1; slot=2/1/0 → SLW/SoW/Regen.

opcode.name: OP_BuffWindow
opcode.comment: EQ Legends buff-window init (S>C, 12B = buffWindowSlotStruct, fires 10x/zone-in for slots 0-9). CONFIRMED 2026-07-08: 10 fires, slot u32@0 = 0..9, spellid u32@4 = 0xffffffff (empty), pad u32@8 = 0 — byte-identical to Live. NOTE: no OP_BuffWindow handler is wired on eql yet (id-only; resolves in opcode-stats but not surfaced). Layout: {u32 slot, u32 spellid=0xffffffff_empty, u32 pad}; long-duration buffs come via OP_Buff / the buff-bar sync; never fires mid-session.

opcode.name: OP_HouseAddress
opcode.comment: House and guildhall address information - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_HouseContents
opcode.comment: Server sending house information and contents - variable length

opcode.name: OP_FriendsList
opcode.comment: Server sending friends list contents - variable length (Sent when choosing "Welcome" from EQ button)

opcode.name: OP_FriendsOnline
opcode.comment: Friends currently online - variable length (Sent when choosing "Welcome" form EQ button)

opcode.name: OP_Rewards
opcode.comment: Server sending pending rewards - variable length

opcode.name: OP_FTPNags
opcode.comment: Free to play nags and other data - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_Find
opcode.comment: Find window data - variable length, 24 min

opcode.name: OP_ConsiderCorpse
opcode.comment: ConCorpseCode - 20 bytes

opcode.name: OP_TributeUpdate
opcode.comment: Tribute and trophy skills - Variable length

opcode.name: OP_Unknown1
opcode.comment: 3 byte opcode that spam logs seemingly only when you have someone targeted. Marked here to make reading logs easier

opcode.name: OP_Unknown2
opcode.comment: 3 byte opcode that spam logs seemingly only when you have someone targeted. Marked here to make reading logs easier

opcode.name: OP_Unknown3
opcode.comment: 3 byte opcode that spam logs seemingly only when you have someone targeted. Marked here to make reading logs easier

opcode.name: OP_RequestZoneChange
opcode.comment: Send by server when a click causes zone. Also, origin

opcode.name: OP_SendAATable
opcode.comment: Static AA ability-definition burst sent S>C on zone-in — ONE record per packet (fires once per AA rank; ~1008x in the fight capture across resends). Variable-length 130..346B = a 37B fixed head (matches Live aaInfoStruct: descID@0, titleSID@13, cost@25, aaID@29, rank@33) + a variable prereq/effect tail. EqlDispatch::sendAATable reads descID@0 + titleSID@13; titleSID -> dbstr_us.txt type-1 = the AA display name. See eqldispatch.cpp / OPCODES_LEGENDS.md.

opcode.name: OP_AckPacket
opcode.comment: Appears to be generic ack at the presentation level

opcode.name: OP_Weather
opcode.comment: old WeatherCode

opcode.name: OP_ReqNewZone
opcode.comment: Client requesting NewZone_Struct

opcode.name: OP_ReqClientSpawn
opcode.comment: Client requesting spawn data

opcode.name: OP_SendExpZonein
opcode.comment: Both directions. Negotiating sending of exp info.

opcode.name: OP_AAAction
opcode.comment: Dual-form: C>S 16b aaActionStruct {action, ability, unknown, exp_value} for buy/activate (action=3=buy), plus S>C 12b companion server messages (fire frequently). Re-confirmed post-2026-05-22 patch (aaaction-spend): the single C>S 16b buy fire decoded action=3 ability=224 (Mental Clarity rank purchase) alongside ~100 S>C 12b companions.

opcode.name: OP_SetServerFilter
opcode.comment: Client telling server /filter information

opcode.name: OP_GuildTributeStatus
opcode.comment: Guild tribute stats send on zone

opcode.name: OP_GuildTributeInfo
opcode.comment: Guild tribute loadout, only if on

opcode.name: OP_RespawnWindow
opcode.comment: Server telling client enough to populate the respawn window when you die

opcode.name: OP_InitialMobHealth
opcode.comment: Initial health sent when a player clicks on the mob. Subsequent updated will be OP_MobHealth

opcode.name: OP_MobHealth
opcode.comment: health sent when a player clicks on the mob

opcode.name: OP_DeltaCheck
opcode.comment: Client sending server delta information.

opcode.name: OP_LoadSpellSet
opcode.comment: /mem spellsetname

opcode.name: OP_Consume
opcode.comment: Client force feeding food/drink

opcode.name: OP_LFGCommand
opcode.comment: old LFGReqCode

opcode.name: OP_Bug
opcode.comment: /bug

opcode.name: OP_Save
opcode.comment: Client asking server to save user state

opcode.name: OP_Camp
opcode.comment: old cStartCampingCode

opcode.name: OP_ShopPlayerSell
opcode.comment: old SellItemCode

opcode.name: OP_ShopEnd
opcode.comment: old CloseVendorCode

opcode.name: OP_SenseHeading
opcode.comment: old cSenseHeadingCode

opcode.name: OP_LootComplete
opcode.comment: old sDoneLootingCode

opcode.name: OP_ShopRequest
opcode.comment: old OpenVendorCode

opcode.name: OP_FaceChange
opcode.comment: /face

opcode.name: OP_Sneak
opcode.comment: Clicked sneak

opcode.name: OP_DisarmTraps
opcode.comment: Clicked disarm traps

opcode.name: OP_Forage
opcode.comment: old ForageCode

opcode.name: OP_GMKill
opcode.comment: GM /kill - Insta kill mob/pc

opcode.name: OP_ShopPlayerBuy
opcode.comment: old BuyItemCode

opcode.name: OP_LFPCommand
opcode.comment: looking for players

opcode.name: OP_ConfirmDelete
opcode.comment: Client sends this to server to confirm op_deletespawn

opcode.name: OP_TargetCommand
opcode.comment: Target user

opcode.name: OP_FindPersonRequest
opcode.comment: Control-F Find

opcode.name: OP_FindResponse
opcode.comment: Response to control-F

opcode.name: OP_GMLastName
opcode.comment: GM /lastname - Change user lastname

opcode.name: OP_TributeInfo
opcode.comment: Tribute information

opcode.name: OP_RespondAA
opcode.comment: Server response to OP_AAAction; carries 12B records (ability_id, rank, _) for player's full AA list incl. auto-grants and purchased ranks

opcode.name: OP_HideCorpse
opcode.comment: 07/07 rotation (community f-patch id, daemon-confirmed): C>S 4B corpse-hide toggle. Present 18×4B C>S in upperguk capture. Identification-only, no consumer. Named for logs.
